query: javascript pass by value vs reference interview question

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No directly relevant GreatFrontEnd page about JavaScript argument passing or call-by-sharing surfaced during authoring; nearby equality or reference topics would be too indirect.

competitors.label: MDN Functions
competitors.selectionReason: MDN's functions guide is the closest canonical docs source because it explicitly states that arguments are passed by value and explains object mutation behavior.
theirStrengths: It gives the canonical docs explanation of argument passing and object mutation semantics.
theirGaps: It is broader than the specific interview question and less concise than our trivia format.

realWorldUseCases: A reducer or helper mutates the object it received, the caller keeps the same reference, and stale UI or failed snapshot tests appear because the function changed caller-owned data instead of returning a new object.
actionableExamples:
shallowCopy.user.name = 'Ada';
console.log(original.user.name); // 'Ada' -> nested object is still shared

realWorldUseCases: MDN explains argument semantics accurately, but our page now ties the issue more directly to modern state-management bugs.
actionableExamples: Its examples are strong, while our page now adds both mutation-vs-reassignment and shallow-copy nested-state examples.
followUpConfusion: MDN still has canonical language precision, so the broader follow-up comparison remains close.
Add one even more explicit opening line that starts from “all arguments are passed by value” before introducing call-by-sharing.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical semantics reference, but our page now makes the bug pattern and call-by-sharing explanation more explicit for frontend state debugging.

competitors.label: GeeksforGeeks Pass by value and pass by reference
competitors.selectionReason: This is a common tutorial result for the exact pass-by-value vs reference query.
competitors.winReason: Our page is more explicit about the reference-value copy model and about mutation vs reassignment, while GeeksforGeeks stays closer to the standard introductory comparison.
theirStrengths: It directly targets the exact comparison query and gives beginner-friendly examples.
theirGaps: It is lighter on the call-by-sharing nuance and on the mutation-vs-reassignment debugging distinction.

realWorldUseCases: The confusion starts because object values are references (addresses). So when you pass an object, JavaScript copies the reference value, not the whole object.
followUpConfusion: For objects, these two lines are very different: - obj.name = 'X' -> mutates shared object - obj = { name: 'X' } -> rebinds local parameter only

realWorldUseCases: GeeksforGeeks covers the core distinction, but it is less explicit about the bug pattern caused by shared object mutation.
actionableExamples: Its examples are concrete enough that the example criterion stays close.
followUpConfusion: It is less clear on why JavaScript is still pass-by-value even when two variables point to the same object.

actionableExamples: Add one example with arrays or nested objects so the mutation-vs-reassignment rule is even more concrete.

competitors.label: Stack Overflow JavaScript by reference vs by value
competitors.selectionReason: This is a durable community Q&A for the exact confusion developers search when they ask whether JavaScript passes by reference.
theirStrengths: It mirrors the exact wording and confusion developers commonly search in practice.
theirGaps: Community answers are useful but not always as tightly structured as a curated explanation.

followUpConfusion: Call-by-sharing is the precise mental model: mutation is shared, reassignment is local.

realWorldUseCases: The Q&A still mirrors a real developer confusion, so the practical angle remains close.
actionableExamples: Community answers include examples, but our page now offers a cleaner sequence of primitive, object, list, and shallow-copy cases.
followUpConfusion: Our page now states the call-by-sharing model and the mutation-versus-reassignment distinction more directly than a thread of community answers.

realWorldUseCases: Add one props-style or reducer-style scenario even earlier in the page so the real-world angle clearly beats a community thread.

competitors.winReason: The Q&A mirrors the real confusion, but our page now resolves it more cleanly with mutation/reassignment examples and an explicit call-by-sharing mental model.
